outlook.headline: NAND layer scaling stays disciplined, with tight supply extending
outlook.summary: Higher NAND layer counts are still being pulled by AI memory demand, which keeps conversion activity and material intensity supportive for suppliers like Lam Research and Entegris. SanDisk’s read on limited greenfield and measured bit growth argues against an oversupply reset, while Advanced Energy remains only a pass-through beneficiary until spending broadens.

drivers.label: AI-driven higher layer counts
drivers.detail: Rising NAND layer counts are being driven by AI memory demand, lifting tool intensity and keeping the scaling cycle active.

drivers.label: Tight supply / disciplined expansion
drivers.detail: Supply remains tight and layer-count-driven bit growth is measured, suggesting capacity is not expanding aggressively enough to create near-term oversupply.

drivers.label: Material intensity rises with scaling
drivers.detail: Moving to higher layer counts increases materials usage per unit of output, supporting consumables demand.

drivers.label: Limited greenfield additions
drivers.detail: SanDisk flagged limited new greenfield supply, which helps keep the supply side controlled over the next 1-2 quarters.

drivers.label: Broad spending still uneven
drivers.detail: Advanced Energy only saw a passing reference to NAND conversion spending, implying the upcycle is not yet broad-based across all suppliers.

risks: Layer-count conversion spending stays incremental instead of broadening to more suppliers.
NAND makers accelerate greenfield or bit-growth plans faster than expected, easing tightness.
AI memory demand slips, reducing the urgency for higher-layer scaling investments.
